finding: After `node atm.mjs evidence git-head-backfill ...` and a governed evidence-only follow-up commit, the worktree can briefly report `.atm/history/evidence/git-head.jsonl` as modified again even though the evidence commit itself already succeeded. The file settles back to clean, but the transient dirty state is confusing and looks like residue or a second required evidence commit.
expectedBehavior: Evidence-only follow-up commits should either suppress redundant post-commit git-head rewrites or surface a precise "transient auto-refresh" diagnostic so operators do not misread the worktree as genuinely dirty.
evidenceOrRepro: Reproduced and then covered on 2026-06-28 by isolating evidence-only follow-up commit fixtures and sampling git status until the transient rewrite settles. The lane now has deterministic regression coverage in `scripts/validate-governance-commands.ts`, which verifies the evidence-only follow-up commit path and guards against stale mixed-scope assumptions without requiring a human to interpret brief worktree flicker.
followUp: Keep the evidence-only follow-up fixture isolated from mixed-scope commit tests, and preserve the settle-check sampling so timing-sensitive residue regressions stay visible without turning the validator into a flaky sleep-heavy lane.
